prompt: Search for the keyword 'scrt' in the src directory and keep only the most recent matches with a linear window curve.
Save the search results from the previous query into a mind‑palace slot named 'search-scrt-main' with a note explaining its purpose.

arguments.name: search-scrt-main
arguments.note: Results of searching for the keyword scrt in the main src directory, recent matches only.

prompt: Find stashes similar to 'search-scrt-main' that share at least some content and list the top 5.
Search for the regex pattern 'parseArgs' in the src directory with normal effort and recent sorting.
Save the search results from the previous query into a mind‑palace stash named 'parse-args-search' with a note and tags.

arguments.name: parse-args-search
arguments.note: Occurrences of parseArgs in src directory

prompt: Retrieve the stash 'parse-args-search' including the captured node bodies.

Search for the regex `pub struct RawArgs` in the src directory, limit to 20 nodes and 500 tokens, sort by relevance, and store the results in a stash named `raw-args-search` with note 'RawArgs definition' and tags ['parser', 'struct'].
Save the previous search results into a mind‑palace slot named `raw-args-search` with note 'RawArgs definition', tags ['parser','struct'], and set it to replace any existing stash of that name.
Find stashes similar to `raw-args-search` using the note axis, with a tight score of 8, and return only the top 3 matches.

Search for the regex pattern `\bparseArgs\b` in the src directory, limit to 20 nodes and 500 tokens, sort by recency with a linear window curve, then stash the results into a mind‑palace slot named `arg-parser-search` with note `Results of parseArgs search`, tags `search,parser`, and set replace to true.
Save the previously searched nodes into a stash named `arg-parser-search` with note `Results of parseArgs search`, tags `search,parser`, and replace any existing stash with that name.
Find stashes similar to the stash `arg-parser-search` using note similarity, tighten the score to 8 for a narrower match, and return only the top 3 neighbors.

Search for the definition of `RawArgs` in the codebase, capture the relevant lines, and store them in a mind‑palace slot named `raw-args-definition` with tags `rust`, `cli-parser`. Use a normal effort and recent sorting.
Save the search results from the previous call into a stash called `raw-args-definition` with note "Definition of RawArgs struct in args.rs" and tags `rust`, `cli-parser`. Merge if it already exists.

arguments.name: raw-args-definition
arguments.note: Definition of RawArgs struct in args.rs

prompt: Find stashes similar to the `raw-args-definition` stash, focusing on note similarity with a tight score of 8 and returning only the top 5 results.

Search for the regex pattern `use scrt_core::format` in the src directory and store the results in a mind‑palace slot named `core-format-search`, tagging it with `search` and `rust`. The search should be normal effort, sorted by relevance, and include only 20 nodes.

Save the previous search results into a mind‑palace slot called `core-format-search`, with note "Search for format module usage", tags `search` and `rust`. Replace any existing slot with the same name.
List all mind‑palace slots that have the tag `search` and retrieve the full node bodies of the slot named `core-format-search`.
Retrieve the stash `core-format-search` including its captured nodes.
Drop the mind‑palace slot named `core-format-search` because it is no longer needed.
Find stashes similar to the raw query "parseArgs" using note similarity with a tight score of 8, returning only the top 5 matches.
search for the phrase "user login" in a palace file and compose a query
stash the last search results under the name "login-stash"
list all stashes, retrieve the one named "login-stash", and drop it afterwards while also finding similar stashes to a given node id
command: scrt list-stashes && scrt get-stash "login-stash" && scrt drop-stash "login-stash" && scrt find-similar --mp-similar 42

prompt: Search for the login function and stash the results
List all stashes and retrieve the one named my-login-stash
Drop a stash called old-stash and find similar stashes to my-login-stash
command: scrt drop-stash old-stash --mp-drop_stash && scrt search "login" --in src/auth/login.ts --mp-similar my-login-stash

prompt: Search for nodes in the directory `src/auth/login.ts` and compose a mind palace graph
Stash the last 10 results from a previous search into a stash named "login-ideas"
List all stashes, retrieve the stash named "login-ideas", and drop it afterwards
command: scrt list-stashes && scrt get-stash login-ideas && scrt drop-stash login-ideas
